MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Jun-04-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORTY, SCOTT HUNTER, SARA RAMLER and BARBARA SMITH's MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS:

Filed to be heard one court day before trial, defendants' motion for judgment on the pleadings is continued to be heard by the trial judge. Efficiency will be thus served, as the trial judge will be immersed in the facts and law of the case.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar May-07-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Martin Poggio, Esq. of the Law Office of Vernon C. Goins, 510.663.3700, for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Colin H. Jewell, Esq. of Cholakian & Associates, 650.871.9544, for the Defendants.

Defendants San Francisco Housing Authority, Scott Hunter, Sara Ramler, Barbara Smith's Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

The matter is called. ARGUMENT is held.

At the conclusion of argument, Defendants San Francisco Housing Authority, Scott Hunter, Sara Ramler, Barbara Smith's Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is taken under submission.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

After the hearing, the Court signed the Order Denying Defendants' Motion for Summary Judgment, Denying Summary Adjudication of First, Second, and Third Causes of Action, and Granting Summary Adjudication of Fourth Cause of Action and for Punitive Damages.
